1. Acute ST-elevation myocardial infarction — The patient presents with classic angina symptoms, ST-segment depression in V4–V6, and a significantly elevated troponin I, indicating active myocardial ischemia.
2. Non-ST-elevation myocardial infarction — The ECG shows ST-segment depression and T-wave inversion without Q waves, and the elevated troponin confirms myocardial injury in the setting of ischemic symptoms.
